meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block by building SaaS pricing tools. A beginner's guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship profitable apps fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' block is a myth; you lack a target problem. By building a SaaS pricing calculator, you identify high-value micro-problems for B2B clients. Use Antigravity to scaffold the logic and Claude Code to iterate, turning a simple utility into a repeatable, revenue-generating freelance service.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for potential. You stare at the screen, waiting for a 'billion-dollar idea' that never arrives, while your freelance pipeline gathers dust. The truth? You don't need a revolution; you need a utility. The most successful builders don't wait for inspiration; they engineer systems that solve the mundane, high-friction problems businesses pay to avoid.

SaaS pricing is the ultimate entry point. Every founder struggles to balance margins, CAC, and conversion. By building a sophisticated pricing strategy tool, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a business lever. This is your gateway to high-ticket consulting and the end of the 'no project' paralysis.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the core unit of value (e.g., API calls, seats, storage) that dictates your client's pricing model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component structure for a SaaS pricing slider that calculates MRR based on user-defined tiers and value metrics.

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine
steps.description: Implement the mathematical logic to handle tiered vs. usage-based billing models.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript utility function that calculates annual recurring revenue given monthly active users and tiered subscription plans.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'src/utils/pricingLogic.ts' --spec 'calculate annual revenue based on tiered inputs'

steps.title: Integrate Data Persistence
steps.description: Ensure the calculator remembers client configurations using local storage or a lightweight database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a hook to persist pricing configurations to localStorage with proper error handling and schema validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'src/hooks/usePricingStore.ts' --apply 'persist state to local storage'

steps.title: Add Visual Analytics
steps.description: Visualize the impact of pricing changes using interactive charts to impress your clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Recharts into the dashboard to visualize MRR growth projections based on the pricing model input.

steps.title: Export for Client Handoff
steps.description: Package the tool into a clean, deployable format that clients can immediately use.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Optimize the build for production and generate a README explaining how to integrate the pricing module into an existing Next.js app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run build' && claude write 'README.md' --context 'client-handoff'

pro_tips: Always build the pricing engine as a standalone 'micro-SaaS' so you can sell the same tool to multiple clients in different niches.
Use 'Value-Based Pricing' as your primary calculator feature; clients pay more for tools that help them justify higher prices to their own customers.
Embed a 'Sensitivity Analysis' toggle in your calculator—it allows users to see how churn rates impact their bottom line, which is a major pain point.
Never hardcode pricing tiers; pass them via a JSON config file so your clients can update their pricing without touching the code.
Always include a 'Download PDF Report' button; turning a digital calculation into a physical document significantly increases the perceived value of your work.
content.next_steps: Once you have your pricing calculator built, host it on Vercel and reach out to three local SaaS founders offering it as a free audit tool to get your foot in the door for larger development contracts.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the tools that businesses are desperate for. By mastering SaaS pricing, you become an indispensable asset to your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
